question: Does weighting UCB1 scores by maintenance urgency convert passive display into active dispatch pressure?
expect: dispatch_scoring.py ucb1_score() reads maintenance-actions.json and applies domain modifiers: meta +0.5..+2.0 when DUE/URGENT items exist. Baseline: 0 maintenance influence on dispatch. Target: top-3 dispatch includes meta when URGENT items exist.
actual: Implemented _load_maintenance_urgency() in dispatch_scoring.py. Reads workspace/maintenance-actions.json at import time. Meta domain gets +0.5 per DUE item, +1.0 per URGENT item, capped at +2.0. With 1 DUE item: meta score 5.2→5.7 (still #2). With 3 DUE items (concurrent session state): meta 5.2→6.9 (#1, above nk-complexity 5.7). Module-level loading means zero runtime overhead per domain.
diff: Expected meta in top-3 when URGENT: CONFIRMED (even 1 DUE item keeps meta in top-2). Unexpected: the boost from just DUE items (not URGENT) is sufficient to shift meta to #1 when 3+ items accumulate. The mechanism is proportional — responds to maintenance load, not binary threshold.
self_apply: This weighting IS a swarming function improvement (F-SWARMER1). If sessions are dispatched to fix maintenance because of this weight, the swarming cycle becomes self-correcting.
successor: Validate: does meta dispatch frequency increase in next 10 sessions? Measure pre/post maintenance-actions clear rate.
